purpose: Measured mutation-score floors for the hand-written modules Stryker mutates (generated src/** excluded), including the wrapper authenticated-client factory and final authenticated-dispatch boundary. Floors ratchet monotonic-up; raise only after a real test improvement. The checker validates every contract-changing commit in complete, non-shallow first-parent history and enforces historical maximum floors plus the union of governed packages and module-floor paths against committed and dirty contracts. Root or first-introduction bootstrap is allowed only when complete history proves no earlier contract; shallow repositories and every unexpected Git/read/parse failure fail closed. The sole immutable historical module retirement is commit 0392e6943f9277dc91179328e61dd01d7c3c8d9e, which replaced mcp/src/orchestration/confirm-guard.ts with mcp/src/tool-risk.ts at floor 70; the retired path cannot reappear. New packages and modules may be added, but every other historically governed package and path must remain present with well-formed numeric floors and cannot decrease. Positive mutate source paths and module floors must match exactly, resolve to repository files, and remain outside every configured exclusion; governed modules fail closed when a report contains zero covered mutants. A module floor may be zero only during an explicit, temporary calibration: its exact active source path must appear once in a non-empty calibrationPending list, and every pending source must have floor zero. The global floor may be zero only for the CLI first calibration under its exact globalCalibrationPending flag; no unrelated package may calibrate. Calibration entries and flags must be removed when measured floors are raised. Mutation entrypoints generate the ignored manifest-derived runtime constants before Stryker starts; MCP and CLI build their SDK workspace dependency and pin their dedicated governed-module test files so Vitest discovery cannot silently omit coverage. Phase 1 covers the wrapper hand-written modules. Phase 2 adds the MCP safety-critical modules (confirmation token store, centralized guarded registration, and governed risk registry). Phase 3 covers the CLI command-risk, reference-resolution, and receipt modules at their measured positive floors; retained-run approvals and the Task 18 aggregate proof remain separate closure work.
